recognition: Tuple A/B close via HostFinalizeModuleNamespace
HashMap → IndexMap on Object.properties (ECMA §10.1.11)
Function.name + .length own-property synthesis (ECMA §10.2.9/§10.2.10)
HashMap dedup substrate restores compile-phase linearity on object-literal interning (32k keys: 2243ms → 38ms, 59x). Sentry compile unchanged — refutes intern-loop as its dominant cost.

Rc snapshot of parent locals shared across sibling nested-function compiles. Probe B restored to linear (3200 sibs: 607ms → 24ms, 25.6x). Sentry compile 11.4s → 1.4s (8.1x); eval collateral 15.3s → 5.3s (2.9x); total 26.9s → 7.0s (3.9x).

Derived-class field inits placed after super() call instead of prepended to ctor body. Restores spec-correct InitializeInstanceElements timing per §15.7.13 step 11. Required for Callable-style patterns where parent ctor explicitly returns a non-this object.

Propagate new.target through super(...) via Op::PropagateNewTarget + __super_apply. Co-evolves with class-field-after-super: together recover N-level Callable-style class chains (arktype family). Two-level chains worked accidentally pre-substrate via Op::New's own implicit-return-this fallback.

Tier-1.5 alphabet promotion: Expr::IsSpecObject(v) discriminates §6.1 Type(V)===Object directly. Closes the typeof-null collapse that broke ToPrimitive(null) and any template-literal coercion of null. arktype advanced to next wall (node.js:216:39 parseDefinition).

Class methods + accessors install with {w:t, e:f, c:t} per ECMA §15.7. Object.keys on class prototypes now returns []. Spec-correctness win; does NOT recover arktype's wall-4 manifestation (proto-as-this has a different proximate cause).
.mjs files compile as strict by default per ECMA §15.5. Does not recover arktype (its *.js files already trigger strict via has_module_syntax). Independent spec correctness fix.

super.X getter dispatch passes original this (not super-base) per ECMA §13.3.7.3 + §10.1.7.2. Closes arktype wall 4 root: BaseRoot.get rawIn returning super.rawIn cached the super-base prototype as the value on the prototype itself, leaking through every branch.rawIn access. arktype advances to a new wall further into discriminate (union.js:661, distinct trace).

for-in over undefined/null short-circuits to 0 iterations per ECMA §14.7.5.6 step 6. Identified via Doc 730 §XVI engine-diff probe: bun completes 0 iter, cruftless threw via Object.keys ToObject. Joi loads cleanly post-fix; top500 cluster of 14 expected to recover.

Strict-mode rejection of write-to-non-writable (TypeError) + write-to-undeclared (ReferenceError) per ECMA §10.1.9.4 + §13.15.4. Threads strict from CompiledModule → Frame; gates Op::SetProp/SetIndex on receiver-own-descriptor; gates Op::StoreGlobal on globals.contains_key. Recovers later + ast-types-flow on exemplar-43 (both move to MATCH_OK_ERR_BOTH). §XV audit verdict corroborated: no deviation warranted.
process.execArgv defaults to [] matching Bun + Node API. Recovers resolve-package-path. §XVI probe: case (1) Node-compat L3 surface gap.
util.styleText(format, text) Node-22 stub returns text verbatim. Recovers inquirer. §XVI probe: case (1) Node-compat L3 gap.

CJS body strict defaults to false (was: inherited from ESM wrapper). Matches Node CJS sloppy convention. Recovers icalendar, secp256k1 family, etc. later flips back to load (now diverges from Bun's stricter-than-Node rejection).

var declarations in for/forin/forof init/left position hoisted to function top per ECMA §10.2.11 step 25-26. collect_var_hoists previously skipped init. Surfaced by Ω.5.P04.E2.strict-write-enforcement on bn.js. Co-committed with cjs-wrapper-sloppy-default at 5732cce9.

rusty-js-jit crate scaffold + Cranelift 0.118 integration. Smoke test passes (add(2,3)=5 round-trips through Cranelift JIT module path). Build time on Pi 1m05s. Toolchain wired, ready for Class A op translation work.
Bytecode→Cranelift translator with arith-i64 subset (LoadArg, Add, Sub, Mul, Return, ReturnUndef). Virtual SSA stack at compile time. Verifier rejects unsupported ops. 4 tests pass on Pi (aarch64).

Translator extended to sum(N) bytecode (LoadLocal/StoreLocal, PushI32, comparisons, control-flow via Cranelift Blocks + brif). All 5 tests pass. Bench: JIT sum(1M) = 1.25ms vs interpreter 532ms (425x speedup) vs Bun 3ms (0.42x of Bun). CAVEAT: i64-path only, not spec-faithful Value boxing; demonstration of structural ceiling per Doc 731 §V S1.

Typed-i64 alphabet promotion at the bytecode tier: AddI64/SubI64/MulI64/IncI64/DecI64 + LtI64/LeI64/GtI64/GeI64/EqI64/NeI64. Interpreter dispatches as pure-integer; JIT translates direct to Cranelift. β path per Doc 731 §XIV.d. 6 JIT tests pass, parity-fast clean.

Benched β-path (typed-i64 ops) vs cheat-path (plain ops, i64-assumed) on sum(1M). Both at ~1.3ms (415x vs 386x speedup); difference within noise. β-path costs nothing in performance vs the cheat path; the alphabet promotion is purely architectural cleanliness. Doc 731 §XIV.d corroborated empirically.

JIT-EXT 7: function-level optimistic typed-i64 promotion pass + auto-promote in compile_function. Real JS source → bytecode → auto-promote → JIT achieves 422x speedup on sum(1M). Doc 731 §XIV.f open frontier closed.

End-to-end JIT in call_function. ClosureInternals.call_count + Runtime.jit_cache + Runtime.jit_threshold (CRUFTLESS_JIT_THRESHOLD env). Guard: hot + arity 1/2 + all-Number-integer args. sum(1M) interpreter 522ms → JIT 2ms (261x speedup, 1.5x faster than Bun's 3ms). callSum/propSum unchanged (function bodies not typed-i64-eligible). Parity-fast clean.
